name: Satellite Management
description: End-to-end satellite operations management — track orbital paths, monitor real-time telemetry, plan and execute missions, manage launch schedules, and coordinate docking at ground and space stations.
author: AENEWS Auto-Generator

settings.label: Default Tracking Interval (seconds)
settings.description: Interval between automatic orbital position updates for all tracked satellites

settings.label: Telemetry Alert Threshold (%)
settings.description: Deviation percentage from nominal values that triggers a telemetry alert notification

settings.label: Auto-Log Telemetry
settings.description: Automatically log all incoming telemetry data to persistent storage without manual approval

settings.label: Maximum Active Missions
settings.description: Upper limit on the number of concurrently active missions allowed per tenant

events.description: A new orbit registration has been created with full Keplerian elements and propagation model
New telemetry data has been received from a satellite downlink and ingested into the system
A mission has transitioned to active status and crew have begun executing the mission plan
A launch has been initiated with liftoff confirmed and vehicle is on its planned trajectory
A spacecraft has completed docking procedures with a station and hard-mate is confirmed

agent.name: Satellite Discovery Agent
agent.description: Auto-derived agent from the discovery engine that analyzes satellite plugin data to provide intelligent insights, anomaly detection recommendations, and operational summaries across orbits, telemetry, missions, launches, and stations.

knowledge.title: Satellite Management Overview
knowledge.content: The Satellite Management plugin provides comprehensive end-to-end oversight of orbital operations within the AENEWS platform. It manages five core domains: Orbits (orbital tracking with Keplerian elements and propagation), Telemetry (real-time sensor data ingestion and monitoring), Missions (mission planning, crew management, and execution tracking), Launches (launch scheduling, vehicle configuration, and flight profiles), and Stations (ground and space station management with module tracking and docking coordination). Together these domains enable full situational awareness of all satellite assets and operations.

knowledge.title: Satellite Management FAQ
knowledge.content: Q: How do I track a new satellite orbit? A: Navigate to Orbits and click Create — provide the satellite ID and Keplerian elements. Q: What triggers telemetry alerts? A: When any sensor reading deviates beyond the alert_threshold setting percentage from nominal. Q: Can I run multiple missions simultaneously? A: Yes, up to max_missions concurrent active missions per tenant. Q: How are launches linked to orbits? A: Each launch specifies a target_orbit_id that the vehicle will inject into. Q: What happens at station docking? A: The station.docked event fires, crew counts update, and docking port status changes.

knowledge.title: Satellite Operations Policy
knowledge.content: All satellite operations follow these operational policies: (1) Orbit data must be refreshed at the configured default_tracking_interval. (2) Telemetry ingestion is governed by the auto_log_telemetry setting — when enabled, all data is automatically persisted. (3) Anomaly detection uses the alert_threshold percentage to flag deviations. (4) Mission creation requires at minimum a mission name, objective type, and assigned commander. (5) Launch operations require a valid vehicle, pad assignment, and open launch window. (6) Station decommissioning triggers debris tracking procedures. (7) Admin-level permission (satellite.admin) is required for system-wide configuration changes.
